problem.title: Count the Number of Complete Components

approaches.title: Initial adjacency matrix approach
approaches.summary: Represents the graph as a boolean adjacency matrix and checks connectivity by comparing rows.

revisions.shortChangeSummary: Initial implementation using a boolean adjacency matrix to track connections and verify complete components.

approaches.title: Bitwise adjacency optimization
approaches.summary: Uses bitwise operations to represent the adjacency matrix, improving memory efficiency and access speed.

revisions.shortChangeSummary: Replaced boolean matrix with u64 bitmasks for adjacency representation and optimized visited tracking.

approaches.title: Early exit bitwise traversal
approaches.summary: Optimizes bitwise traversal by skipping inactive bits and exiting early when a mismatch is found.

revisions.commitComment: escape early if nothing else in byte
revisions.rawCommitSubject: rust 2685: escape early if nothing else in byte
revisions.shortChangeSummary: Implemented active bit tracking with while loop and early exit to reduce unnecessary checks.

approaches.title: Brian Kernighan's optimization with trailing_zeros
approaches.summary: Uses Brian Kernighan's algorithm to efficiently iterate over set bits, reducing traversal overhead.

revisions.rawCommitSubject: rust 2685: Brian Kernighan's optimisation with trailing_zeros()
revisions.shortChangeSummary: Optimized bit iteration using trailing_zeros() and active_bits &= active_bits - 1 for faster set bit processing.

approaches.title: Disjoint Set Union (DSU) approach
approaches.summary: Uses DSU to track connected components and checks for complete components by comparing expected vs actual edges.

revisions.shortChangeSummary: Switched to DSU to manage connected components and validate completeness via edge count formula.
